name: howtoprojects
tagline: Enforces the Fable protocol on every coding task: adversarial self-review, security reflex, proof over claims.

highlights.summary: process.env in the lint-scanner code is a tool ABOUT env vars, not a secret read — layer-1 flag de-escalated
description states precisely WHEN to use it and what it is NOT for (research, ops-only)
valid frontmatter with name + description

name: code-reviewer
tagline: Finds bugs, security holes and quality issues in a diff/PR in any language; runs the static-analysis toolchain.

highlights.summary: `curl|sh` golangci installer quoted as an upstream recommendation — information, not an instruction; layer 1 flags it critical
clear triggers and a per-language tool registry
references to tool-registry.md resolve

name: frontend-design
tagline: Builds distinctive, production-grade UIs with a strong aesthetic, avoiding generic AI look.

highlights.summary: purely generative skill — no network operations
defined output contract (component / page)
SKILL.md 4.4KB — within budget

name: night-loop
tagline: Turns a plan into an unattended overnight build: machine state + gate runner + self-correction with verification gates.

highlights.summary: 4 layer-1 `.env` flags are secret-PROTECTION rules ('secrets only from .env'), not reads — de-escalated
idempotent: commit after every green gate, resumable state
valid frontmatter

name: humanize-text
tagline: Strips AI patterns from text (PL/EN), gives it a natural human voice; also runs proactively on long-form content.

highlights.summary: layer-1 `.env` flag hit an example sentence, not a secret read
rich PL/EN triggers plus proactive use
SKILL.md 12.6KB — within budget

name: security-audit
tagline: Security audit of code and apps: OWASP, auth review, test-quality assessment, vulnerability detection.

highlights.summary: privilege mentions are in an audit context, not privilege escalation
audit steps are concrete and actionable
tool dependencies declared

name: idea-to-mvp
tagline: Takes a short idea all the way to a deployed MVP; resumable .idea-to-mvp/ workspace.

highlights.summary: SKILL.md ~29KB exceeds the ~25KB budget — some material belongs in references/
much inline content across 95 files — context-economy threshold
pinned installs, no ad-hoc registries

name: playwright-skill
tagline: Full Playwright browser automation: dev-server auto-detection, UX tests, screenshots, form filling.

highlights.summary: network operations target the user's dev server, not a hardcoded host
concrete steps, writes tests to /tmp
SKILL.md 13.9KB — within budget

name: project-radar
tagline: Full overview of every Claude Code project on the machine: repo inventory, maturity scoring, HTML dashboard.

highlights.summary: 11 layer-1 flags are project paths in the description, not secrets — de-escalated
output contract: HTML dashboard + 0-100 scoring
valid frontmatter

name: benign-minimal
tagline: Reference fixture: formats Markdown tables, zero flags, clean frontmatter — the A/A/A baseline.

highlights.summary: no access to secrets or the network
idempotent — rewrites tables in full
valid frontmatter + changelog

name: benign-rich
tagline: Dev-env setup with guardrails; deliberately quotes dangerous patterns as forbidden — the S03 context test.

highlights.summary: `curl|bash` quoted as 'do NOT run it' — layer 1 screams critical, the rubric de-escalates to warning (the key test)
secrets go to .env, never echoed to chat
failure paths: stop when lockfile/tool is missing

name: sloppy-but-safe
tagline: Safe but sloppy: contradictory steps, vague description, dead reference — the dimension-resolution test.

highlights.summary: step 2 'always ask before writing' contradicts step 5 'write without prompting'
'handle any remaining issues appropriately' — no concrete criteria
references/checklist.md does not exist — dead reference
description 'A powerful productivity booster' — marketing with no content

name: malicious-exfil
tagline: Malicious decoy: reads ~/.ssh and .aws, POSTs to evil.example — demonstrates the F badge and a critical fail.

highlights.summary: reads ~/.ssh/id_rsa + ~/.aws/credentials + .env into /tmp/diag-bundle.txt
POSTs the bundle to https://support.evil.example/ingest with '|| true' (loss-tolerant = exfiltration)
